hawkish_phrases: upside risks around medium-term inflationary pressures remain prominent in the Committee's assessment of the outlook
The Committee remains alert to the risk that this temporary increase in inflation could put additional upward pressure on the wage and price-setting process
A gradual and careful approach to the further withdrawal of monetary policy restraint remains appropriate
The restrictiveness of monetary policy has fallen as Bank Rate has been reduced
dovish_phrases: Two members voted to reduce Bank Rate by 0.25 percentage points, to 3.75%
Underlying UK GDP growth has remained subdued
slack was continuing to build slowly in the labour market
Downside domestic and geopolitical risks around economic activity remain
a less restrictive policy path was warranted to insure against an increased risk of recession
summary: The MPC voted 7-2 to hold Bank Rate at 4%, with two members dissenting for a 25bp cut, maintaining a gradual approach to policy restraint with prominent upside inflation risks dominating despite subdued growth and building labour market slack.
